H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: VOL001 (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78) leads overstimulation category with 66% positive. Qualitative (n=29) shows balanced activity mix and strong music opinions. YouTube=30 views but no retention data. AutoGluon predicts 6.859 (delta -0.29). Panel divided on YouTube interpretation — potential undiscovered efficacy. Confidence tier strong.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Pebble for overstimulation is the top performer in its category with strong music and rhythm feedback. Some discover it through YouTube.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: VOL001 (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73) shows consistent DESCENT with 65% positive — almost identical to L1. Qualitative (n=21) shows settle time shifted to ~10min. YouTube=5 views. Confidence tier strong — suggest possible L1/L2 merger consideration.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Rainfall for overstimulation performs almost identically to Pebble, raising questions about whether they should be combined.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: VOL001 (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68) shows highest positive rate in category (64%). Qualitative (n=23) indicates strong 'trying to sleep' use (8/23). YouTube=8 views. Confidence tier meaningful — consistent with overstim protocol intent.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Glacier for overstimulation continues the strong pattern with particularly good results for sleep applications.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: VOL001 data (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93) shows strong DESCENT efficacy with 63% positive responses. Qualitative context (n=23) indicates moderate/significant improvement with ~10min settle time. YouTube signal weak (7 views, no retention). AutoGluon predicts 7.84 (delta +0.62) suggesting cross-bucket routing potential to X-ME-L1. Confidence tier strong.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Meadow for anxiety performs strongest in its category with many listeners feeling calm within 10 minutes. Music and rhythm opinions are generally positive.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: VOL001 (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62) shows tighter distribution than L1. 67% positive with mode 7-8. Qualitative (n=20) shows balanced therapy session and driving use, with music opinion mixed (9 liked, 5 neutral). Confidence tier meaningful — recommend continued monitoring.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Cottage for anxiety works well in therapy settings with steady outcomes. Listeners find it helpful but sometimes prefer other options.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: VOL001 (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89) maintains DESCENT success pattern. 61% positive but lower n limits confidence. Qualitative shows 10min settle time dominant. AutoGluon not available for this protocol. Confidence tier meaningful but early.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Valley for anxiety maintains consistent patterns similar to other anxiety protocols. Data is still developing but shows promise.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: VOL001 (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.7) shows expected crisis-state variability with 53% positive. Qualitative (n=24) shows strong music reception (16 liked/loved) despite acute starting conditions. YouTube has 6 views. Confidence tier meaningful — structural underperformance expected.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Anchor for crisis states works despite intense starting conditions. Some listeners feel immediate effects with good music reception.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: VOL001 (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.7) shows 55% positive with earlier settle times than L1. Qualitative (n=16) indicates mixed music opinions (6 neutral, 5 loved). YouTube views=4. Confidence tier meaningful — consistent with crisis protocol design.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Harbor for crisis shows mixed results with some finding it helpful quickly while others don't respond as strongly.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: VOL001 (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91) mirrors L1 mean but shows wider distribution. Qualitative (n=19) reveals fastest immediate settling (7/19) but also 4/19 not liked music response. YouTube=5 views. Confidence tier developing — accumulating data.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Horizon for crisis matches other crisis protocols but has limited data. More responses would help understand its effects.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: VOL001 (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86) leads sleep category with 65% positive and faster settling profile. Qualitative (n=15) shows therapy session use predominance. YouTube=8 views. AutoGluon predicts 7.539 (delta +0.35). Confidence tier strong.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Lantern for sleep leads the category with fastest settling times and strong therapist recommendation patterns.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: VOL001 (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55) shows tightest distribution in sleep sub-series. Qualitative (n=15) reveals higher clinician usage (6/15 client/patient). Music opinion split (6 neutral, 5 liked). YouTube=5 views. Confidence tier meaningful.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Hammock for sleep shows tightest distribution with stronger clinical use. Some listeners find it too busy.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: VOL001 (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68) maintains pattern with lower n. Qualitative (n=22) shows balanced settling (5min/10min). YouTube=3 views. Confidence tier developing — needs more data.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Midnight for sleep mirrors other sleep protocols but needs more data to confirm patterns.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: VOL001 (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82) shows expected focus maintenance with 56% positive. Qualitative (n=23) shows studying activity predominance. YouTube=5 views. AutoGluon similarity 0.948 with L2. Confidence tier meaningful.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Compass for focus shows moderate improvements with typical settle times around 5-10 minutes.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: VOL001 (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.6) shows tighter focus profile with 66% positive. Qualitative (n=17) indicates caregiver/therapy use balance. YouTube=4 views. Confidence tier meaningful — good anchor protocol.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Waypoint for focus performs better with tighter responses and balanced use cases.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: VOL001 (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97) shows new focus pattern with 71% positive — highest in THRIVING. Qualitative (n=29) indicates 11/29 significant improvement with 15min settle. YouTube=15 views. AutoGluon predicts 7.728 (delta +0.10). Notable embedding drift (0.576). Panel divided on drift significance. Confidence tier meaningful.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Summit for focus is the standout performer with the highest satisfaction and significant improvement reports.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: VOL001 (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73) shows baseline creative flow. 53% positive with early settle times. Qualitative (n=21) shows balanced activity mix. YouTube=2 views. AutoGluon cluster confirms shared efficacy with L3. Confidence tier meaningful.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Ember for creative flow works as a baseline tool with most listeners settling quickly.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: VOL001 (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41) shows tightest creative flow distribution. Qualitative (n=15) indicates highest 'loved it' music response (6/15). YouTube=2 views. Confidence tier meaningful — weekend preparation pattern noted.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Current for creative flow shows the tightest responses with high satisfaction rates.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: VOL001 (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32) shows strongest creative tool with highest mean in series. Qualitative (n=25) indicates 7/25 significant improvement. YouTube=7 views. AutoGluon similarity 0.953 with L1 confirms shared architecture. Confidence tier developing — limited n.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Aurora for creative flow is the strongest in its category with many reporting significant improvements.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: VOL001 (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34) shows good baseline with 64% positive. Qualitative (n=23) indicates high clinician usage (8/23 therapy session). YouTube=3 views. Confidence tier meaningful.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Stone for maintenance works well as a baseline with strong clinical usage patterns.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: VOL001 (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03) shows wider variance. 59% positive. Qualitative (n=16) shows balanced activity. YouTube=4 views. Confidence tier meaningful — longer session use indicated.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Axis for maintenance shows varied responses with longer session use indicated.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: VOL001 (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73) shows developing profile with 71% positive but n=14. Qualitative (n=19) indicates 4/19 did NOT settle. YouTube=3 views. Confidence tier developing — needs expansion.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Orbit for maintenance shows promise but needs more data; some listeners don't settle at all.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: VOL001 (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94) shows strongest mood/energy with 54% positive. Qualitative (n=22) shows balanced music opinions. YouTube=8 views. AutoGluon predicts 8.230 (delta +1.49) — top low-affect score. Cross-bucket routing to multiple protocols. Confidence tier meaningful.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Breeze for mood has hidden potential according to predictions — may benefit from rhythm adjustments.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: VOL001 (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71) confirms L1 pattern with 64% positive. Qualitative (n=15) shows highest 'loved it' music (6/15). YouTube=8 views. AutoGluon predicts 8.230 (same as L1). Confidence tier meaningful.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Sunrise for mood confirms the pattern with strong music responses despite similar outcomes.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: VOL001 (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74) shows 56% positive with 5/25 no effect. Qualitative (n=25) shows 9/25 very settling rhythm. YouTube=4 views. Confidence tier developing — late settling indicated.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Updraft for mood shows some listeners don't respond, suggestingPossible design mismatch.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: VOL001 (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01) shows 45% positive — lowest in motivation category. Qualitative (n=17) shows 9/17 settle ~5min. YouTube=5 views. AutoGluon predicts 7.876 (delta +1.52). Cross-bucket routing to X-ME-L1. Confidence tier meaningful — bimodal indicated.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Footpath for motivation has mixed results that vary significantly between different listener groups.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: VOL001 (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63) shows 43% positive. Qualitative (n=21) shows 5/21 ~20min settle. YouTube=7 views. Confidence tier developing — expanding n needed.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Trail for motivation continues the mixed pattern with longer settling times for some.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: VOL001 (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38) shows highest in category with 62% positive but n=13. Qualitative (n=17) shows 8/17 moderate improvement. YouTube=7 views. Confidence tier developing — wide std noted.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Switchback for motivation shows highest positive rates despite wide variance in responses.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: VOL001 (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62) shows solid low-state clarity with 54% positive. Qualitative (n=25) shows balanced 'loved it' music (9/25). YouTube=8 views. AutoGluon similarity 0.951 with L2. Confidence tier meaningful.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Cavern for mind clarity works solidly with late settling but strong music appreciation.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: VOL001 (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64) shows stronger than L1. 56% positive with faster settling. Qualitative (n=20) indicates 7/20 significant improvement. YouTube=12 views. Confidence tier developing.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Starlight for mind clarity outperforms its predecessor with faster settling and significant improvements.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: VOL001 (n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45) shows critical underperformance — lowest in library. 50% positive with 0/10 'love it' music. Qualitative (n=17) shows 4/17 'too busy' rhythm opinion. YouTube=8 views. AutoGluon predicts 7.538 (delta +1.84). Multiple analyzers flag for immediate redesign. Confidence tier early data — high uncertainty.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Cosmos for mind clarity is the weakest protocol overall with some finding it too busy — likely needs redesign.

sphere_commentary.overview:
Across 1000 outcome scores, HEALING and THRIVING maintain aligned efficacy (6.98 and 6.96 means) while TRANSFORMING trails at 6.59 — consistent with its low-affect state design.
• HEALING protocols (n=574) show robust regulation with lowest variance (std=1.77)
• THRIVING (n=245) maintains similar mean but higher confidence through HOLD protocols
• TRANSFORMING (n=181) exhibits expected spread fromascending-arc design (std=1.87)
• 10 protocols have n<20 requiring cautious interpretation
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
The three series separate by therapeutic architecture and listener population stress profiles.
• HEALING DESCENTs dominate efficacy (6.35-7.22 range) with crisis variants structurally lower
• THRIVING HOLD protocols cluster tightly (6.46-7.63) with Focus & Clarity leading
• TRANSFORMING ASCENT protocols show widest spread (5.7-7.15) indicating entrainment challenges
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Bimodal distributions and low-n effects warrant investigation.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45) diverges critically from series trend — potential design mismatch
• T-FC-L3 shows notable embedding drift (0.576 cosine) suggesting evolving interpretation
• 12/30 protocols have n<50 — pattern uncertainty applies
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Actionable protocol development opportunities identified.
[CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 requires immediate redesign — multiple analyzers flag low mean, high variance, and bimodal distribution as critical
[CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L3 warrants visibility increase — strong AutoGluon predicted score (7.73) exceeds observed (7.63)
Cross-bucket routing in X-ME-L1 suggests rhythm reconfiguration for entrainment improvement
H-OS-L1 YouTube retention data gap (30 views, no duration) limits reach signal interpretation
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Analytical stability shows medium consistency with notable exceptions.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: T-FC-L3 at 0.576 similarity shows 42% analytical shift since 2026-04-02 — requires investigation
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: T-CF-L1 ↔ T-CF-L3 cluster at 0.953 confirms shared efficacy architecture
• Low similarity protocols (H-CR-L3, T-FC-L3, H-OS-L1 at ~0.657 mean) indicate analytically distinct subgroups
